description_long: This is a vintage cathode-ray tube (CRT) television with a large, convex glass screen set within a rectangular wooden cabinet. The front features a wide black bezel surrounding the screen, with a control panel on the right side that includes various buttons, dials, and a speaker grille below. The wood grain finish gives the television a classic, retro appearance, and the back is constructed from black plastic with multiple ventilation slots, a central protruding tube housing, and several input/output ports. The sides are flat with a continuation of the wood grain, and one side has a small vent. The overall build is bulky and heavy, typical of older television sets, and the top surface is flat and could support lightweight objects.
description: A bulky, vintage CRT television with a wood grain cabinet, convex glass screen, and a side control panel with buttons and speaker grille. The back is black plastic with vents and ports, and the overall design is retro and robust.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex glass screen, wood grain bezel, and a detailed control panel with buttons and speaker grille on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the flat wood grain panel and the depth of the television's cabinet.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the black plastic back with ventilation slots, central tube housing, and various input/output ports.
description_view_3: Right side view with a wood grain panel and a small vent, highlighting the television's thickness.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick wood grain bezel with a black inner border, rectangular shape, and a control panel integrated on the right side.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of buttons and dials on the front right panel, arranged vertically above the speaker grille.
